title: ING agile transformation
subtitle: From traditional banking IT to tribes and squads at scale
source_label: ING Newsroom, McKinsey agile at scale interviews
context: ING restructured technology and operations around agile squads inside tribes. Public reporting describes faster delivery and stronger customer focus after moving away from siloed waterfall style planning.
before_summary: Department silos, long release cycles, slow response to digital competitors.
after_summary: Cross functional squads, shorter feedback loops, measurable uplift in delivery cadence.

kpis.label: Employee engagement
kpis.unit: internal surveys cited in case material

changes: Introduced tribes and squads instead of pure functional departments
Reduced management layers for faster decisions
Continuous delivery mindset on digital channels
Coaching for leaders on servant leadership

sprints.goal: Pilot squad model on mobile banking slice
sprints.notes: Legacy approval gates still outside squad control

sprints.goal: Expand squads and align tribe backlog
sprints.notes: Shared platform team became explicit dependency

sprints.goal: Steady release train on customer facing app
sprints.notes: Impediments escalated to tribe lead within 48h rule

retros.action: Escalate external approvals in daily when idle 2 days
glad: Squad had real end to end ownership on UI slice
sad: Still waiting on legacy ops handoffs

retros.action: Single tribe backlog view for conflicting priorities
glad: Faster decisions when PO and engineers co located
mad: Too many stakeholders in sprint review
